meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Build Faster as a Freelancer
content.seo_description: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate. Use this expert API integration guide to ship client projects faster with Antigravity and Claude Code. Start now.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development cycles, standardize your API integration layer using an abstraction-first approach. By leveraging Antigravity for automated boilerplate generation and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can cut integration time by 70%. Focus on decoupled architecture to ensure your code remains maintainable, scalable, and highly performant.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and you're still stuck debugging a third-party API response schema. Every hour spent manually mapping JSON objects is an hour subtracted from your bottom line. The bottleneck isn't the API—it's the friction between your intent and the implementation.

We don't code like it's 2015 anymore. By shifting to an AI-augmented workflow, you stop being a syntax monkey and start being an architect. This guide bridges the gap between complex external documentation and production-ready code, allowing you to ship high-impact features while your competitors are still reading documentation.

steps.title: Define the Interface Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define the API contract. This prevents type mismatch errors and ensures your frontend and backend stay in sync.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface and Zod schema for an API response based on this raw JSON payload: [PASTE_PAYLOAD]. Ensure strict type safety and include custom error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a new interface file in /src/types from the provided JSON schema'

steps.title: Generate the API Client
steps.description: Use an abstraction layer to encapsulate authentication and request logic. This makes swapping endpoints painless later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Axios or Fetch wrapper that handles Bearer token injection, request logging, and global error interceptors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement an api client factory that handles auth headers and base configurations'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Retries
steps.description: Real-world APIs fail. Build resilience into your client using exponential backoff to handle rate limits and transient errors gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add an interceptor to my API client that implements exponential backoff for 429 and 5xx errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add retry logic to the existing api client using axios-retry or equivalent'

steps.title: Automated Mocking for Development
steps.description: Don't hit live endpoints during local development. Use mocks to simulate various API states, including edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock server configuration using MSW (Mock Service Worker) that mimics the API endpoints defined in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure msw for all routes in the new api service'

steps.title: Final Integration and Testing
steps.description: Connect your new client to the UI and verify data flow. Use Claude Code to audit the integration for potential memory leaks or performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test suite for the API integration that covers success scenarios and common failure modes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit the integration for security risks and performance bottlenecks'

pro_tips: Always use Zod for runtime validation; TypeScript types alone won't save you from malformed API responses.
Store API keys in environment variables and never commit them; use a .env.example file to keep your team aligned.
Implement request caching using TanStack Query to prevent redundant network calls and improve perceived performance.
Use 'cURL to Fetch' converters to instantly turn API documentation examples into usable code snippets.
Keep your API service files modular—one file per resource domain (e.g., 'users.service.ts', 'payments.service.ts').
content.next_steps: Once your integration is live, set up automated monitoring to track API latency and error rates. If you haven't already, package your client as a private NPM module to reuse across all your future freelance projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a pro. Your time is your most valuable asset, and we are here to help you protect it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
